key_insight: PNOT reconstructs divertor temperature fields by combining graph-based boundary physics, physics-aware attention, and gradient-constrained learning to improve accuracy and physical consistency.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more generalizable real-time thermal monitoring and control of tungsten monoblock divertors, helping reduce overheating, melting risk, and component damage in fusion devices.
method: Models boundary heat-flux relations as a structured graph with graph attention to capture spatial physical dependencies.
Uses a physics-aware neural operator module to group query points with similar physical conditions and model heat diffusion via slicing.
Adds Sobolev regularization to enforce consistency between predicted temperature values and their spatial derivatives.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the proposed physical constraints improve prediction accuracy while preserving physical consistency for divertor temperature-field reconstruction.
watch_item: The approach depends on carefully designed physics priors and may need validation for different divertor geometries, operating regimes, or sensor coverage patterns.
paper_title: Temperature Field Reconstruction of Tungsten Monoblock Divertor on EAST using Physics-aware Neural Operator Transformer
